title: Truth Finish It Campaign
summary: The campaign that ended the teen smoking epidemic.

body.text: We partnered with The Truth Initiative to launch the Finish It campaign in August 2014 as a complete revolution of its long running truth anti tobacco efforts. Facing a new generation of youth and young adults who had grown less responsive to traditional industry manipulation messaging, the campaign shifted focus to empowerment and collective action. It targeted 15 to 21 year olds with the rallying cry to make their generation the one that finally ends smoking. Research showed this audience craved positive social change rather than protest, so Finish It positioned them as agents of progress. The campaign connected tobacco use to issues young people cared about such as relationships, social justice, money, and personal freedom while using a modern visual identity and digital first approach.
The campaign prevented an estimated 301,930 from becoming smokers.

The creative strategy centered on anthemic video manifestos like Finishers that declared we have the power, we have the creativity, we will be the generation that ends smoking. Finish it. Youth were encouraged to join the movement by overlaying the orange X logo on their social profiles and sharing user generated content. Multi channel tactics included partnerships with influencers, events like the MTV Video Music Awards launch, and integrations that tied into pop culture. The campaign amplified earned media and created a sense of community around ending the tobacco epidemic. By highlighting progress points such as campuses going smoke free or states raising the tobacco purchase age, it provided tangible proof that collective action worked.

The Finish It campaign achieved strong public health results. Independent studies linked higher campaign awareness to improved anti tobacco attitudes, reduced smoking intentions, and measurable declines in youth smoking rates. A key 2017 study found that the campaign prevented an estimated 301,930 youth and young adults from becoming smokers during 2015 to 2016 alone. This built on the broader truth campaigns legacy of preventing hundreds of thousands of young people from starting to smoke and saving hundreds of thousands of lives overall since 2000. Teen smoking rates dropped substantially following the launch from around nine percent in 2014 toward historic lows. Overall it advanced the goal of making this the generation that finishes smoking while demonstrating the power of branded youth centric public education.
